---
title: "Cascadia Medical Devices"
subtitle: "Predictive maintenance and OEE analytics on a 6M-row manufacturing dataset."
---

::: {.case-study-header}
::: {.cs-domain}
Manufacturing Analytics
:::
# Cascadia Medical Devices
::: {.cs-hook}
Proving that a production-grade BI architecture scales from raw sensor data to boardroom OEE dashboards — in a single, reproducible build.
:::
:::

---

## Overview

<!-- TODO (Cowork): 2–3 sentence overview. -->
::: {.todo-block}
**TODO (Cowork — Overview):** 2–3 sentences: medical device manufacturing domain, the core business question (OEE / downtime / predictive maintenance), and the headline skill demonstrated (SQL Server star schema design + Microsoft Fabric medallion pipeline).
:::

---

## Business Problem

<!-- TODO (Cowork): Framing the manufacturing analytics problem. -->
::: {.todo-block}
**TODO (Cowork — Business Problem):** Frame the problem: a medical device contract manufacturer needs real-time visibility into equipment downtime, quality defect rates, and production throughput. What decisions does the Power BI report enable? What would a BI leader at a manufacturing company actually care about?
:::

---

## Architecture

Every Cascadia domain uses the same end-to-end stack:

::: {.arch-container}
![Cascadia end-to-end data platform](../assets/cascadia_analytics_tech_stack.svg)
:::

**Source layer:** CascadiaMES SQL Server star schema — 7 dimension tables + 3 fact tables (fact_production_event 5.93M rows, fact_downtime_event 6.5K rows, fact_quality_qn 41K rows, fact_order_fulfillment). Simulated MES data + NASA C-MAPSS engine sensor dataset (265K rows) for predictive maintenance.

**Medallion pipeline:** Bronze → Silver → Gold notebooks in Microsoft Fabric Lakehouse, implemented in PySpark.

**Semantic model + report:** Power BI dataset with DAX measures for OEE, MTBF, defect rate, and on-time delivery.

---

## Data Sources

| Source | Type | Rows | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| CascadiaMES (simulated) | SQL Server | ~6M | 7 dims + 3 facts, star schema |
| NASA C-MAPSS | Public (NASA) | 265K engine cycles | Turbofan engine sensor data for RUL prediction |

---

## Headline Skill: SQL Server Star Schema Design

<!-- TODO (Cowork): 1–2 paragraphs on the schema design decisions. -->
::: {.todo-block}
**TODO (Cowork — Headline Skill):** 1–2 paragraphs explaining the key schema decisions: surrogate key strategy, date_key computed column pattern (why post-load ALTER TABLE?), why FKs are omitted on facts (enforced at semantic model layer), and how the 3-fact design separates production events from quality events for clean DAX measures. Make it interview-conversational — "here's what I decided and why."
:::

---

## The Report

<!-- TODO (Cowork + Aaron): Screenshots of the Power BI report go here. -->
::: {.todo-block}
**TODO (Aaron — Report screenshots):** Add 1–3 screenshots of the Power BI report to `assets/img/med-devices-report-*.png`. Then replace this block with:
```markdown
![OEE Dashboard](../assets/img/med-devices-report-oee.png)
![Downtime Analysis](../assets/img/med-devices-report-downtime.png)
```
Optionally embed a walkthrough video (Loom/YouTube) and/or a "View live report" button once Power BI embedded link is available.
:::

---

## Tech Stack

::: {.tech-chips}
::: {.chip}SQL Server 2019:::
::: {.chip}T-SQL:::
::: {.chip}Python (simdata):::
::: {.chip}Microsoft Fabric:::
::: {.chip}PySpark:::
::: {.chip}Power BI:::
::: {.chip}DAX:::
::: {.chip}PowerShell:::
:::

---

## Links

- [Build Repository](https://github.com/RobbinsAnalytics/manufacturing-analytics) — SQL DDL, data generation scripts, medallion notebooks
- [Architecture Explainer](https://github.com/RobbinsAnalytics/manufacturing-analytics/blob/main/docs/explainers/02_source_sql_layer.md) — schema design decisions and data dictionary
- [Cascadia Architecture Overview](../cascadia.qmd)
